metadata.title: Privacy Policy | LIFO
metadata.description: Privacy Policy for LIFO food waste management platform

section1.title: 1. Who We Are
section1.content: LIFO provides batch-level inventory management software for retail stores. We help you track product expiration dates, reduce food waste, and make data-driven decisions about discounts and donations. Contact Us: Email: contact@lifo-app.com | For GDPR rights requests: contact@lifo-app.com

section2.title: 2. What Data We Collect
section2.intro: We collect the following types of data:
items: Account Information: Name, email address, store name and location, user role (admin, manager, or employee), login credentials (encrypted)
Inventory Data: Product names and descriptions, batch numbers and quantities, expiration dates, barcode information, discount and donation decisions
Product Images: Photos captured during barcode and expiration date scanning, processed through image recognition technology, used solely for extracting product information and dates
Usage Data: Actions taken within the app (scans, updates, decisions), timestamps of activities, device information (browser type, operating system, IP address), performance and error logs

section3.title: 3. How We Use Your Data
items: Provide Our Service: Enable inventory tracking and management, generate discount and donation recommendations, create analytics dashboards showing inventory value and waste metrics, send important notifications about expiring products
Improve Our Service: Enhance image recognition accuracy, optimize recommendation algorithms, identify and fix technical issues, develop new features based on usage patterns
Communicate With You: Send service updates and important announcements, respond to your support requests, notify you of changes to our terms or policies
Comply With Legal Obligations: Meet food safety and waste reporting requirements, respond to legal requests when required, protect against fraud and abuse

legalBasis.title: We process your data based on:
items: Performance of Contract: To provide the Service you signed up for
Legitimate Interests: To improve our Service, prevent fraud, and ensure security
Your Consent: Where we explicitly request permission (e.g., for optional features)
Legal Obligation: To comply with applicable laws and regulations

section5.title: 5. Data Storage and Security
items: Where We Store Your Data: All data is stored on secure servers located in the European Union. We use Supabase (EU region) for database hosting. Data is encrypted both in transit (TLS/SSL) and at rest.

How We Protect Your Data: Industry-standard encryption protocols, regular security audits and vulnerability assessments, access controls and multi-factor authentication, secure development practices and code reviews, employee training on data protection.

Data Retention: We retain your data for as long as your account remains active. Upon account deletion, we delete or anonymize your data within 90 days. Some data may be retained longer if required by law (e.g., accounting records).

storage.title: We do not sell your personal data.
storage.content: We may share data only with: Service Providers (Cloud Hosting: Supabase - EU-based, GDPR-compliant; Image Processing: Google Vision API for expiration date recognition; Email Services: Resend or similar for account notifications; Analytics: Usage analytics to improve the Service), for Legal Requirements (comply with legal obligations or court orders, protect our rights or prevent fraud, investigate security incidents, respond to law enforcement requests), or With Your Consent (for other purposes only with your explicit permission).

retention.title: Third-Party Processors
retention.content: All third-party processors are contractually bound to GDPR standards, process data only according to our instructions, and implement appropriate security measures.

section7.title: 7. Your Privacy Rights (GDPR)
section7.intro: Under GDPR, you have the right to:
items: Access Your Data: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you
Rectify Inaccurate Data: Correct any information that is incomplete or incorrect
Delete Your Data ("Right to Be Forgotten"): Request deletion of your personal data (subject to legal retention requirements)
Data Portability: Receive your data in a structured, commonly used format (CSV, JSON)
Object to Processing: Object to certain data processing activities (e.g., direct marketing)
Restrict Processing: Limit how we process your data in specific circumstances
Withdraw Consent: Withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent (does not affect prior processing)
section7.contact: To exercise these rights, contact us at
section7.responseTime: We will respond to your request within 30 days (or 60 days for complex requests, with notification).

section8.title: 8. Cookies and Tracking Technologies
section8.intro: We currently use Essential Cookies (required for Service): authentication tokens (keeps you logged in), session management (maintains your active session), security cookies (prevents unauthorized access). We may implement additional cookies for:
items: Performance monitoring and analytics
User preference storage (e.g., language settings)
Service improvement and A/B testing
section8.outro: You will be notified before we introduce new cookie types. You can control cookie preferences through your browser settings. Note: Disabling essential cookies may limit Service functionality.

section9.title: 9. International Data Transfers
section9.intro: Your data is primarily stored in the European Union. If we transfer data outside the EU, we ensure adequate protection through:
items: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the EU Commission
Adequacy Decisions (for countries recognized as providing adequate protection)
Other lawful transfer mechanisms under GDPR Article 46
section9.outro: We will notify you of any changes to our data transfer practices.

section10.title: 10. Data Breach Notification
section10.content: In the unlikely event of a data breach affecting your personal data, we will notify you within 72 hours of becoming aware and inform relevant supervisory authorities as required by GDPR. We will provide information about the nature of the breach, data potentially affected, likely consequences, measures taken to address the breach, and steps you can take to protect yourself.

section11.title: 11. Children's Privacy
section11.intro: The LIFO Service is not intended for individuals under 16 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If we discover that we have inadvertently collected data from a child, we will delete it immediately.
items: If you believe we have collected data from a child, please contact us at: contact@lifo-app.com

section12.title: 12. Automated Decision-Making
section12.content: We use automated systems to generate discount and donation recommendations based on product expiration dates, historical sales patterns, inventory levels, and store-specific data. Important: These recommendations are advisory only. You retain full control over all business decisions. We do not make automated decisions with legal or significant effects without human oversight.

section13.title: 13. Changes to This Privacy Policy
section13.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, legal or regulatory updates, or new features or services. We will notify you of material changes via email to your registered address, in-app notification, or notice on our website. Continued use of the Service after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

section14.title: 14. Your Data Protection Authority
section14.intro: You have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority if you believe we have violated GDPR or your privacy rights.

section15.title: 15. Data Ownership
section15.content: You own your data. All inventory data, product information, and business insights generated through LIFO remain your property. We are merely processors of this data on your behalf. You can export your data at any time in standard formats (CSV, JSON) through your account settings.

section16.title: 16. Contact Us
section16.intro: For questions about this Privacy Policy, to exercise your GDPR rights, or for any privacy concerns:
section16.responseTime: We aim to respond to all privacy inquiries within 2 business days.

finalStatement.content: By using LIFO, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy.
